claims.claim: The 2026 osek patur revenue threshold is ₪122,833
claims.note: Confirmed via WebSearch May 2026. Kol Zchut publishes the annual threshold; cited as supporting reference (CC BY-NC-SA — do not paraphrase content, just cite the figure). Original authority: rashut hamisim (gov.il). Cross-reference: cwsisrael 2026 freelancer guide confirms the ₪120k baseline + annual indexation.

claims.claim: Annual income tax return (Form 1301) is due April 30
claims.note: PwC's Israel individual tax summary states the standard April 30 deadline with extensions available. The Tax Authority's own gov.il page reconfirms; PwC chosen as the citable secondary source because it remains stable across years.

claims.claim: VAT cadence threshold: revenue above ₪1,615,000 in the 12 months ending August 31 triggers monthly filing; below stays bi-monthly
claims.note: Standard Israeli VAT cadence threshold. PwC's corporate tax administration summary is the cleanest reference.

claims.claim: Keren hishtalmut atzmai maximum annual contribution for tax-exempt profits is ₪20,566 in 2026
claims.note: Route 38 published the 2026 figure in January 2026. Cross-checked with multiple Israeli CPA blogs reporting the same number. The deductibility cap is separate: 4.5% of income up to ₪264,000 = max ₪11,880 deductible (chapter 4 will cite this).

claims.claim: Osek murshe charges 18% VAT on all invoices
claims.note: Israeli standard VAT rate is 18% as of 2025-2026 (raised from 17% in January 2025). Multiple sources confirm.

claims.claim: An osek patur who crosses the threshold mid-year must convert to osek murshe within 30 days and is retroactively liable for VAT on invoices issued at or above the threshold
claims.note: Dray & Dray (Israeli CPA firm) published guide on osek patur threshold crossing. The 30-day conversion window and retroactive VAT liability are standard Tax Authority rules under the VAT Law. Authority: VAT Law (חוק מס ערך מוסף).
